title: Redesigning the court process for cheque-bounce cases
desc: Supriya Sankaran and Ayushi Singhal from Agami share the work PUCAR community members have been doing to redesign the dispute resolution process for cheque bounce cases.

title: Court Cause Lists as a Digital Public Good
desc: Surya Prakash BS from DAKSH shares his work on court cause lists as a digital public good.

title: Measuring courts from the litigant's point of view
desc: Pavithra Manivannan and Bhargavi Zaveri-Shah of XKDR Forum on how court data can empower litigants when interacting with the justice system.

title: The What, Why & How of Restorative Justice
desc: Swagata Raha (Enfold India), Nimisha Srivastava and Arti Mohan (Counsel to Secure Justice), and Sachi Maniar (Ashiyana Foundation), frontrunners in restorative practice, unpack restorative justice.

title: How might law and justice innovations instill trust amongst their users?
desc: Anushka Ashok on the behavioural science of creating trust: frameworks that spark imagination in building trust and its markers.

title: Multi ODR Systems
desc: Entrepreneurs from CADRE, CORD, JustAct, Presolv360, Sama, and Webnyay explore the evolution of ODR, how multi-ODR systems work, and their challenges and opportunities.

title: Bhangjadiya System for Resolution
desc: Traditional justice systems like the Bhangjadiya caste panchayat have long been male-dominated. The women of Mahila Mazdoor collectives are changing that.

title: Multi Actor Platforms: A Pathway to Preventing Conflict
desc: At the heart of every thriving community is the ability to listen, learn, and act together. How multi-actor platforms turn tension into trust.

title: Tareekh Pe Justice
desc: Tracing the everyday journeys of litigants through India's district courts, and what those journeys reveal about the system.
